summary: Stellar soundboard recording of the legendary 9/20/90 MSG show – crystal‑clear audio captures a marathon Dark Star and fire‑blazing second set.
review: The consensus among reviewers is that this performance ranks among the very best of the post‑Brent era, highlighted by an epic Dark Star (the longest modern‑era version) plus standout jams like China→Rider, Althea, Ramble On Rose and a ripping Throwing Stones.  The soundboard source is praised as “out‑of‑this‑world” and “crisp,” with only minor complaints about early Vince synth moments, making the recording as compelling as the show itself.

ai_rating.rationale: Over a dozen detailed 5‑star reviews praise both performance (70% weight) and audio fidelity (30% weight).  Consistently high ratings and specific accolades give a strong, near‑perfect score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers describe the sound as crisp, out‑of‑this‑world and the best of the ’90s; only occasional annoyance with early Vince synth parts. No dropout or balance issues reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Both sets are described as high‑energy and tightly flowing, with a first set that builds momentum and a second set that peaks at the massive Dark Star/Playin' sequence before cruising through superb jams like China→Rider and Throwing Stones.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Often noted for an energized, soaring guitar tone; voice described as a bit weak but still powerful.
band_member_comments.Phil: His bass lines are called “thumping tremendously” and “rattling the floors,” providing a solid foundation throughout.
band_member_comments.Keys: Mixed feelings: early Vince synth moments were irritating to some, but later his piano work (and Bruce Hornsby’s) is praised as a high point of the show.
